claims.statement: MESA-like stellar shell profiles can be imported as profile-validation inputs for StarSim fusion estimates.

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA). Astrophysical Journal Supplement Series.
sources.note: MESA is the preferred profile source family for stellar-structure imports.

claims.statement: Imported shell profiles can estimate nuclear luminosity by integrating epsilon_nuc over shell mass.

constraints: requires shell mass or reconstructable radius-density basis

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA).
sources.note: Stellar profile and energy-generation context for mass-shell integration.

claims.statement: PP, CNO, and triple-alpha channel fractions are profile diagnostics computed from integrated component epsilon contributions.

sources.citation: Adelberger, E. G. et al. (2011). Solar fusion cross sections II: the pp chain and CNO cycles.
sources.note: Reference for pp-chain and CNO-cycle nuclear astrophysics context.

claims.statement: Fusion-zone radii such as r10, r50, and r90 are cumulative-luminosity profile diagnostics.

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA).
sources.note: Profile energy-generation context for cumulative luminosity diagnostics.

claims.statement: Integrated nuclear luminosity and surface luminosity are related profile diagnostics but should not be treated as automatically identical in imported fixtures.

sources.citation: Paxton, B. et al. (2011). Modules for Experiments in Stellar Astrophysics (MESA).
sources.note: Stellar structure context for comparing profile luminosity and observable luminosity.

claims.statement: Neutron-star profile fixtures are compact-object dense-matter context and must not be interpreted as PP/CNO fusion profiles.

sources.citation: Khomenko, V. et al. (2020). Turbulent, pinned superfluids in neutron stars and pulsar glitch recoveries.
sources.note: Compact-object quantum-fluid context for neutron-star glitches.

claims.statement: hSpectralFit metadata is calibration-only because the revised SI fixes the Planck constant exactly.

sources.note: Reference for the exact revised-SI value of the Planck constant.

claims.statement: Gaia-like star-map data can support population priors, not direct quantum-spacetime evidence.

sources.citation: Gaia Collaboration (2023). Gaia Data Release 3: Summary of the content and survey properties.
sources.note: Gaia DR3 observational backbone for astrometric population priors.

claims.statement: StarSim profile import can strengthen astrophysical priors but cannot directly support ER=EPR or promote QST/warp claims to CL0-CL4.

validityDomain.system: StarSim profile import to QST boundary

sources.citation: Maldacena, J. and Susskind, L. (2013). Cool horizons for entangled black holes.
sources.equation: ER=EPR context only; no stellar-profile equation is asserted.
sources.note: Boundary source for keeping ER=EPR claims separate from StarSim profile imports.
